title: Patra Tebut
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image= |name=Patra Tebut |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] ('''75'''), [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Dark |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] **[[Galactic Alliance Guard]] |masters= |apprentices= }} '''Patra Tebut''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] watch [[Officer/Legends|officer]] on the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Sir...can't...loyal..."
"LOYAL?! How dare you use that word?! You may not say that word ever again. Loyal officers do not betray their command, their comrades, their oaths!|Darth Caedus berates Patra Tebut, just seconds before killing her|''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''}} Shortly after the [[Battle of Gilatter VIII|battle of]] [[Gilatter VIII]], Patra Tebut was working on the ''Anakin Solo'' when [[Ben Skywalker]] docked the [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|Sith Meditation Sphere]] he had recovered while on his mission to [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] in the Anakin Solo's hangar bay. [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] ordered her to remove all traces of the ship from the records, and that nobody had seen it, it hadn't docked, and for most purposes, the sphere did not exist. She complied quickly and effectively, leading Solo to muse that like [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Lon Shevu]], Tebut was utterly reliable. Following the escape of the ''Anakin Solo'' from [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] after their [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|attack]] on that world, Tebut became an executive officer candidate, and began training on all of the bridge stations present on the ''Anakin Solo''. It was Tebut who discovered that the Jedi had placed a tracking beacon on the cloak that Darth Caedus, once Jacen Solo, had been wearing. While upset that he had allowed the Jedi to plant a tracer on his cloak, Caedus was pleased with Tebut's performance, and ordered the ship's Captain [[Kral Nevil]] to place a commendation in her permanent record. A short time later, the ''Anakin Solo'' encountered General [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu's]] shuttle, apparently being chased by a squadron of [[Confederation]] X-Wings. The shuttle broadcast all the correct identification codes and passwords, and Tebut allowed the shuttle to land. What she did not know was that General Celchu was not on board the shuttle, instead a strike team consisting of Jedi and their allies were on board. The Jedi infiltrated the ''Anakin Solo'', and were able to successfully rescue [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka's]] daughter [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]] from the ship. After the Jedi had departed, Darth Caedus returned to the bridge and demanded to know who had allowed the shuttle to dock. Tebut admitted that she had allowed the shuttle to dock, but that the shuttle had broadcasted all the correct codes and passwords. Caedus, however, disagreed and began [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|choking]] her with the Force. Captain Nevil attempted to stop him, only to be telekinetically hurled against a bulkhead. In her last moments, Tebut protested her loyalty to Caedus, but only succeeded in enraging him to the point where he was literally seeing red. Finally, after vociferously accosting her for her failure, Caedus snapped her neck and unceremoniously slammed her down onto the deck and stormed off the bridge. This act, committed in such anger, truly bound Caedus to the ways of his grandfather, Darth Vader. Caedus's Co-Chief of State, Admiral [[Cha Niathal]], was alarmed at Tebut's murder and began considering having Caedus removed from power. However at this point Niathal was still unwilling to overtly oppose Caedus, and refused to have Caedus taken out immediately. Niathal later informed [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] of the nature of Tebut's death and that his nephew was responsible; Skywalker was not particularly surprised that this had happened. Caedus himself would later regret his outburst, having deprived himself of a valuable officer and in turn harming his reputation amongst the ranks.''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' {{1st}} *[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'' *[[Legacy of the Force: Fury (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Fury'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' {{Mo}} {{1stID|Patra Tebut}} *[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Revelation'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Invincible'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tebut, Patra}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet personnel]] [[Category:Galactic Alliance Guard personnel]] [[Category:Humans]]
